verses.sanskrit: सूत उवाचनिशम्य कौषारविणोपवर्णितंध्रुवस्य वैकुण्ठपदाधिरोहणम् । प्ररूढभावो भगवत्यधोक्षजेप्रष्टुं पुनस्तं विदुर: प्रचक्रमे ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: sūta uvācaniśamya kauṣāraviṇopavarṇitaṁdhruvasya vaikuṇṭha-padādhirohaṇamprarūḍha-bhāvo bhagavaty adhokṣajepraṣṭuṁ punas taṁ viduraḥ pracakrame
verses.text: Sūta Gosvāmī, continuing to speak to all the ṛṣis, headed by Śaunaka, said: After hearing Maitreya Ṛṣi describe Dhruva Mahārāja’s ascent to Lord Viṣṇu’s abode, Vidura became very much enlightened in devotional emotion, and he inquired from Maitreya as follows.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचके ते प्रचेतसो नाम कस्यापत्यानिसुव्रत ।कस्यान्ववाये प्रख्याता: कुत्र वा सत्रमासत ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācake te pracetaso nāmakasyāpatyāni suvratakasyānvavāye prakhyātāḥkutra vā satram āsata
verses.text: Vidura inquired from Maitreya: O greatly advanced devotee, who were the Pracetās? To which family did they belong? Whose sons were they, and where did they perform the great sacrifices?

verses.sanskrit: मन्ये महाभागवतं नारदं देवदर्शनम् ।येनप्रोक्त: क्रियायोग: परिचर्याविधिर्हरे: ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: manye mahā-bhāgavataṁnāradaṁ deva-darśanamyena proktaḥ kriyā-yogaḥparicaryā-vidhir hareḥ
verses.text: Vidura continued: I know that the great sage Nārada is the greatest of all devotees. He has compiled the pāñcarātrika procedure of devotional service and has directly met the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

verses.sanskrit: स्वधर्मशीलै: पुरुषैर्भगवान् यज्ञपूरुष: ।इज्यमानो भक्तिमता नारदेनेरित: किल ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: sva-dharma-śīlaiḥ puruṣairbhagavān yajña-pūruṣaḥijyamāno bhaktimatānāradeneritaḥ kila
verses.text: While all the Pracetās were executing religious rituals and sacrificial ceremonies and thus worshiping the Supreme Personality of Godhead for His satisfaction, the great sage Nārada described the transcendental qualities of Dhruva Mahārāja.

verses.sanskrit: यास्ता देवर्षिणा तत्र वर्णिता भगवत्कथा: ।मह्यं शुश्रूषवे ब्रह्मन् कार्त्स्‍न्येनाचष्टुमर्हसि ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: yās tā devarṣiṇā tatravarṇitā bhagavat-kathāḥmahyaṁ śuśrūṣave brahmankārtsnyenācaṣṭum arhasi
verses.text: My dear brāhmaṇa, how did Nārada Muni glorify the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and what pastimes were described in that meeting? I am very eager to hear of them. Kindly explain fully about that glorification of the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचध्रुवस्य चोत्कल: पुत्र: पितरि प्रस्थिते वनम् ।सार्वभौमश्रियं नैच्छदधिराजासनं पितु: ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācadhruvasya cotkalaḥ putraḥpitari prasthite vanamsārvabhauma-śriyaṁ naicchadadhirājāsanaṁ pituḥ
verses.text: The great sage Maitreya replied: My dear Vidura, when Mahārāja Dhruva departed for the forest, his son, Utkala, did not desire to accept the opulent throne of his father, which was meant for the ruler of all the lands of this planet.

verses.sanskrit: स जन्मनोपशान्तात्मा नि:सङ्ग: समदर्शन: ।ददर्श लोके विततमात्मानं लोकमात्मनि ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa janmanopaśāntātmāniḥsaṅgaḥ sama-darśanaḥdadarśa loke vitatamātmānaṁ lokam ātmani
verses.text: From his very birth, Utkala was fully satisfied and unattached to the world. He was equipoised, for he could see everything resting in the Supersoul and the Supersoul present in everyone’s heart.

verses.sanskrit: आत्मानं ब्रह्म निर्वाणं प्रत्यस्तमितविग्रहम् ।अवबोधरसैकात्म्यमानन्दमनुसन्ततम् ॥ ८ ॥अव्यवच्छिन्नयोगाग्निदग्धकर्ममलाशय: ।स्वरूपमवरुन्धानो नात्मनोऽन्यं तदैक्षत ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: ātmānaṁ brahma-nirvāṇaṁpratyastamita-vigrahamavabodha-rasaikātmyamānandam anusantatam
verses.text: By expansion of his knowledge of the Supreme Brahman, he had already attained liberation from the bondage of the body. This liberation is known as nirvāṇa. He was situated in transcendental bliss, and he continued always in that blissful existence, which expanded more and more. This was possible for him by continual practice of bhakti-yoga, which is compared to fire because it burns away all dirty, material things. He was always situated in his constitutional position of self-realization, and he could not see anything else but the Supreme Lord and himself engaged in discharging devotional service.

verses.sanskrit: जडान्धबधिरोन्मत्तमूकाकृतिरतन्मति: ।लक्षित: पथि बालानां प्रशान्तार्चिरिवानल: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: jaḍāndha-badhironmatta-mūkākṛtir atan-matiḥlakṣitaḥ pathi bālānāṁpraśāntārcir ivānalaḥ
verses.text: Utkala appeared to the less intelligent persons on the road to be foolish, blind, dumb, deaf and mad, although actually he was not so. He remained like fire covered with ashes, without blazing flames.

verses.sanskrit: मत्वा तं जडमुन्मत्तं कुलवृद्धा: समन्त्रिण: ।वत्सरं भूपतिं चक्रुर्यवीयांसं भ्रमे: सुतम् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: matvā taṁ jaḍam unmattaṁkula-vṛddhāḥ samantriṇaḥvatsaraṁ bhūpatiṁ cakruryavīyāṁsaṁ bhrameḥ sutam
verses.text: For this reason the ministers and all the elderly members of the family thought Utkala to be without intelligence and, in fact, mad. Thus his younger brother, named Vatsara, the son of Bhrami, was elevated to the royal throne, and he became king of the world.

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्वीथिर्वत्सरस्येष्टा भार्यासूत षडात्मजान् ।पुष्पार्णं तिग्मकेतुं च इषमूर्जं वसुं जयम् ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: svarvīthir vatsarasyeṣṭābhāryāsūta ṣaḍ-ātmajānpuṣpārṇaṁ tigmaketuṁ caiṣam ūrjaṁ vasuṁ jayam
verses.text: King Vatsara had a very dear wife whose name was Svarvīthi, and she gave birth to six sons, named Puṣpārṇa, Tigmaketu, Iṣa, Ūrja, Vasu and Jaya.

verses.sanskrit: पुष्पार्णस्य प्रभा भार्या दोषा च द्वे बभूवतु: ।प्रातर्मध्यन्दिनं सायमिति ह्यासन् प्रभासुता: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: puṣpārṇasya prabhā bhāryādoṣā ca dve babhūvatuḥprātar madhyandinaṁ sāyamiti hy āsan prabhā-sutāḥ
verses.text: Puṣpārṇa had two wives, named Prabhā and Doṣā. Prabhā had three sons, named Prātar, Madhyandinam and Sāyam.

verses.sanskrit: प्रदोषो निशिथो व्युष्ट इति दोषासुतास्त्रय: ।व्युष्ट: सुतं पुष्करिण्यां सर्वतेजसमादधे ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: pradoṣo niśitho vyuṣṭaiti doṣā-sutās trayaḥvyuṣṭaḥ sutaṁ puṣkariṇyāṁsarvatejasam ādadhe
verses.text: Doṣā had three sons — Pradoṣa, Niśitha and Vyuṣṭa. Vyuṣṭa’s wife was named Puṣkariṇī, and she gave birth to a very powerful son named Sarvatejā.

verses.sanskrit: स चक्षु: सुतमाकूत्यां पत्‍न्यां मनुमवाप ह ।मनोरसूत महिषी विरजान्नड्‌वला सुतान् ॥ १५ ॥पुरुं कुत्सं त्रितं द्युम्नं सत्यवन्तमृतं व्रतम् ।अग्निष्टोममतीरात्रं प्रद्युम्नं शिबिमुल्मुकम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa cakṣuḥ sutam ākūtyāṁpatnyāṁ manum avāpa hamanor asūta mahiṣīvirajān naḍvalā sutān
verses.text: Sarvatejā’s wife, Ākūti, gave birth to a son named Cākṣuṣa, who became the sixth Manu at the end of the Manu millennium. Naḍvalā, the wife of Cākṣuṣa Manu, gave birth to the following faultless sons: Puru, Kutsa, Trita, Dyumna, Satyavān, Ṛta, Vrata, Agniṣṭoma, Atīrātra, Pradyumna, Śibi and Ulmuka.

verses.sanskrit: उल्मुकोऽजनयत्पुत्रान्पुष्करिण्यां षडुत्तमान् ।अङ्गं सुमनसं ख्यातिं क्रतुमङ्गिरसं गयम् ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ulmuko ’janayat putrānpuṣkariṇyāṁ ṣaḍ uttamānaṅgaṁ sumanasaṁ khyātiṁkratum aṅgirasaṁ gayam
verses.text: Of the twelve sons, Ulmuka begot six sons in his wife Puṣkariṇī. They were all very good sons, and their names were Aṅga, Sumanā, Khyāti, Kratu, Aṅgirā and Gaya.

verses.sanskrit: सुनीथाङ्गस्य या पत्नी सुषुवे वेनमुल्बणम् ।यद्दौ:शील्यात्स राजर्षिर्निर्विण्णो निरगात्पुरात् ॥ १८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sunīthāṅgasya yā patnīsuṣuve venam ulbaṇamyad-dauḥśīlyāt sa rājarṣirnirviṇṇo niragāt purāt
verses.text: The wife of Aṅga, Sunīthā, gave birth to a son named Vena, who was very crooked. The saintly King Aṅga was very disappointed with Vena’s bad character, and he left home and kingdom and went out to the forest.

verses.sanskrit: यमङ्ग शेपु: कुपिता वाग्वज्रा मुनय: किल ।गतासोस्तस्य भूयस्ते ममन्थुर्दक्षिणं करम् ॥ १९ ॥अराजके तदा लोके दस्युभि: पीडिता: प्रजा: ।जातो नारायणांशेन पृथुराद्य: क्षितीश्वर: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: yam aṅga śepuḥ kupitāvāg-vajrā munayaḥ kilagatāsos tasya bhūyas temamanthur dakṣiṇaṁ karam
verses.text: My dear Vidura, when great sages curse, their words are as invincible as a thunderbolt. Thus when they cursed King Vena out of anger, he died. After his death, since there was no king, all the rogues and thieves flourished, the kingdom became unregulated, and all the citizens suffered greatly. On seeing this, the great sages took the right hand of Vena as a churning rod, and as a result of their churning, Lord Viṣṇu in His partial representation made His advent as King Pṛthu, the original emperor of the world.

verses.sanskrit: विदुर उवाचतस्य शीलनिधे: साधोर्ब्रह्मण्यस्य महात्मन: ।राज्ञ: कथमभूद्दुष्टा प्रजा यद्विमना ययौ ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: vidura uvācatasya śīla-nidheḥ sādhorbrahmaṇyasya mahātmanaḥrājñaḥ katham abhūd duṣṭāprajā yad vimanā yayau
verses.text: Vidura inquired from the sage Maitreya: My dear brāhmaṇa, King Aṅga was very gentle. He had high character and was a saintly personality and lover of brahminical culture. How is it that such a great soul got a bad son like Vena, because of whom he became indifferent to his kingdom and left it?

verses.sanskrit: किं वांहो वेन उद्दिश्य ब्रह्मदण्डमयूयुजन् ।दण्डव्रतधरे राज्ञि मुनयो धर्मकोविदा: ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ vāṁho vena uddiśyabrahma-daṇḍam ayūyujandaṇḍa-vrata-dhare rājñimunayo dharma-kovidāḥ
verses.text: Vidura also inquired: How is it that the great sages, who were completely conversant with religious principles, desired to curse King Vena, who himself carried the rod of punishment, and thus awarded him the greatest punishment [brahma-śāpa]?

verses.sanskrit: नावध्येय: प्रजापाल: प्रजाभिरघवानपि ।यदसौ लोकपालानां बिभर्त्योज: स्वतेजसा ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: nāvadhyeyaḥ prajā-pālaḥprajābhir aghavān apiyad asau loka-pālānāṁbibharty ojaḥ sva-tejasā
verses.text: It is the duty of all citizens in a state never to insult the king, even though he sometimes appears to have done something very sinful. Because of his prowess, the king is always more influential than all other ruling chiefs.

verses.sanskrit: एतदाख्याहि मे ब्रह्मन् सुनीथात्मजचेष्टितम् ।श्रद्दधानाय भक्ताय त्वं परावरवित्तम: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: etad ākhyāhi me brahmansunīthātmaja-ceṣṭitamśraddadhānāya bhaktāyatvaṁ parāvara-vittamaḥ
verses.text: Vidura requested Maitreya: My dear brāhmaṇa, you are well conversant with all subjects, both past and future. Therefore I wish to hear from you all the activities of King Vena. I am your faithful devotee, so please explain this.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचअङ्गोऽश्वमेधं राजर्षिराजहार महाक्रतुम् ।नाजग्मुर्देवतास्तस्मिन्नाहूता ब्रह्मवादिभि: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaaṅgo ’śvamedhaṁ rājarṣirājahāra mahā-kratumnājagmur devatās tasminnāhūtā brahma-vādibhiḥ
verses.text: Śrī Maitreya replied: My dear Vidura, once the great King Aṅga arranged to perform the great sacrifice known as aśvamedha. All the expert brāhmaṇas present knew how to invite the demigods, but in spite of their efforts, no demigods participated or appeared in that sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: तमूचुर्विस्मितास्तत्र यजमानमथर्त्विज: ।हवींषि हूयमानानि न ते गृह्णन्ति देवता: ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam ūcur vismitās tatrayajamānam athartvijaḥhavīṁṣi hūyamānānina te gṛhṇanti devatāḥ
verses.text: The priests engaged in the sacrifice then informed King Aṅga: O King, we are properly offering the clarified butter in the sacrifice, but despite all our efforts the demigods do not accept it.

verses.sanskrit: राजन् हवींष्यदुष्टानि श्रद्धयासादितानि ते ।छन्दांस्ययातयामानि योजितानि धृतव्रतै: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājan havīṁṣy aduṣṭāniśraddhayāsāditāni techandāṁsy ayāta-yāmāniyojitāni dhṛta-vrataiḥ
verses.text: O King, we know that the paraphernalia to perform the sacrifice is well collected by you with great faith and care and is not polluted. Our chanting of the Vedic hymns is also not deficient in any way, for all the brāhmaṇas and priests present here are expert and are executing the performances properly.

verses.sanskrit: न विदामेह देवानां हेलनं वयमण्वपि ।यन्न गृह्णन्ति भागान् स्वान् ये देवा: कर्मसाक्षिण: ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: na vidāmeha devānāṁhelanaṁ vayam aṇv apiyan na gṛhṇanti bhāgān svānye devāḥ karma-sākṣiṇaḥ
verses.text: Dear King, we do not find any reason that the demigods should feel insulted or neglected in any way, but still the demigods who are witnesses for the sacrifice do not accept their shares. We do not know why this is so.

verses.sanskrit: मैत्रेय उवाचअङ्गो द्विजवच: श्रुत्वा यजमान: सुदुर्मना: ।तत्प्रष्टुं व्यसृजद्वाचं सदस्यांस्तदनुज्ञया ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: maitreya uvācaaṅgo dvija-vacaḥ śrutvāyajamānaḥ sudurmanāḥtat praṣṭuṁ vyasṛjad vācaṁsadasyāṁs tad-anujñayā
verses.text: Maitreya explained that King Aṅga, after hearing the statements of the priests, was greatly aggrieved. At that time he took permission from the priests to break his silence and inquired from all the priests who were present in the sacrificial arena.

verses.sanskrit: नागच्छन्त्याहुता देवा न गृह्णन्ति ग्रहानिह ।सदसस्पतयो ब्रूत किमवद्यं मया कृतम् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: nāgacchanty āhutā devāna gṛhṇanti grahān ihasadasas-patayo brūtakim avadyaṁ mayā kṛtam
verses.text: King Aṅga addressed the priestly order: My dear priests, kindly tell me what offense I have committed. Although invited, the demigods are neither taking part in the sacrifice nor accepting their shares.

verses.sanskrit: सदसस्पतय ऊचु:नरदेवेह भवतो नाघं तावन् मनाक्स्थितम् ।अस्त्येकं प्राक्तनमघं यदिहेद‍ृक् त्वमप्रज: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: sadasas-pataya ūcuḥnara-deveha bhavatonāghaṁ tāvan manāk sthitamasty ekaṁ prāktanam aghaṁyad ihedṛk tvam aprajaḥ
verses.text: The head priests said: O King, in this life we do not find any sinful activity, even within your mind, so you are not in the least offensive. But we can see that in your previous life you performed sinful activities due to which, in spite of your having all qualifications, you have no son.

verses.sanskrit: तथा साधय भद्रं ते आत्मानं सुप्रजं नृप ।इष्टस्ते पुत्रकामस्य पुत्रं दास्यति यज्ञभुक् ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathā sādhaya bhadraṁ teātmānaṁ suprajaṁ nṛpaiṣṭas te putra-kāmasyaputraṁ dāsyati yajña-bhuk
verses.text: O King, we wish all good fortune for you. You have no son, but if you pray at once to the Supreme Lord and ask for a son, and if you execute the sacrifice for that purpose, the enjoyer of the sacrifice, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, will fulfill your desire.

verses.sanskrit: तथा स्वभागधेयानि ग्रहीष्यन्ति दिवौकस: ।यद्यज्ञपुरुष: साक्षादपत्याय हरिर्वृत: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: tathā sva-bhāgadheyānigrahīṣyanti divaukasaḥyad yajña-puruṣaḥ sākṣādapatyāya harir vṛtaḥ
verses.text: When Hari, the supreme enjoyer of all sacrifices, is invited to fulfill your desire for a son, all the demigods will come with Him and take their shares in the sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: तांस्तान् कामान् हरिर्दद्याद्यान् यान् कामयते जन: ।आराधितो यथैवैष तथा पुंसां फलोदय: ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāṁs tān kāmān harir dadyādyān yān kāmayate janaḥārādhito yathaivaiṣatathā puṁsāṁ phalodayaḥ
verses.text: The performer of the sacrifices [under karma-kāṇḍa activities] achieves the fulfillment of the desire for which he worships the Lord.

verses.sanskrit: इति व्यवसिता विप्रास्तस्य राज्ञ: प्रजातये ।पुरोडाशं निरवपन् शिपिविष्टाय विष्णवे ॥ ३५ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti vyavasitā viprāstasya rājñaḥ prajātayepuroḍāśaṁ niravapanśipi-viṣṭāya viṣṇave
verses.text: Thus for the sake of a son for King Aṅga, they decided to offer oblations to Lord Viṣṇu, who is situated in the hearts of all living entities.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मात्पुरुष उत्तस्थौ हेममाल्यमलाम्बर: ।हिरण्मयेन पात्रेण सिद्धमादाय पायसम् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmāt puruṣa uttasthauhema-māly amalāmbaraḥhiraṇmayena pātreṇasiddham ādāya pāyasam
verses.text: As soon as the oblation was offered in the fire, a person appeared from the fire altar wearing a golden garland and a white dress. He was carrying a golden pot filled with rice boiled in milk.

verses.sanskrit: स विप्रानुमतो राजा गृहीत्वाञ्जलिनौदनम् ।अवघ्राय मुदा युक्त: प्रादात्पत्‍न्या उदारधी: ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa viprānumato rājāgṛhītvāñjalinaudanamavaghrāya mudā yuktaḥprādāt patnyā udāra-dhīḥ
verses.text: The King was very liberal, and after taking permission from the priests, he took the preparation in his joined palms, and after smelling it he offered a portion to his wife.

verses.sanskrit: सा तत्पुंसवनं राज्ञी प्राश्य वै पत्युरादधे ।गर्भं काल उपावृत्ते कुमारं सुषुवेऽप्रजा ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā tat puṁ-savanaṁ rājñīprāśya vai patyur ādadhegarbhaṁ kāla upāvṛttekumāraṁ suṣuve ’prajā
verses.text: Although the Queen had no son, after eating that food, which had the power to produce a male child, she became pregnant by her husband, and in due course of time she gave birth to a son.

verses.sanskrit: स बाल एव पुरुषो मातामहमनुव्रत: ।अधर्मांशोद्भवं मृत्युं तेनाभवदधार्मिक: ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa bāla eva puruṣomātāmaham anuvrataḥadharmāṁśodbhavaṁ mṛtyuṁtenābhavad adhārmikaḥ
verses.text: That boy was born partially in the dynasty of irreligion. His grandfather was death personified, and the boy grew up as his follower; he became a greatly irreligious person.

verses.sanskrit: स शरासनमुद्यम्य मृगयुर्वनगोचर: ।हन्त्यसाधुर्मृगान् दीनान् वेनोऽसावित्यरौज्जन: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: sa śarāsanam udyamyamṛgayur vana-gocaraḥhanty asādhur mṛgān dīnānveno ’sāv ity arauj janaḥ
verses.text: After fixing his bow and arrow, the cruel boy used to go to the forest and unnecessarily kill innocent deer, and as soon as he came all the people would cry, “Here comes cruel Vena! Here comes cruel Vena!”

verses.sanskrit: आक्रीडे क्रीडतो बालान् वयस्यानतिदारुण: ।प्रसह्य निरनुक्रोश: पशुमारममारयत् ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: ākrīḍe krīḍato bālānvayasyān atidāruṇaḥprasahya niranukrośaḥpaśu-māram amārayat
verses.text: The boy was so cruel that while playing with young boys of his age he would kill them very mercilessly, as if they were animals meant for slaughter.

verses.sanskrit: तं विचक्ष्य खलं पुत्रं शासनैर्विविधैर्नृप: ।यदा न शासितुं कल्पो भृशमासीत्सुदुर्मना: ॥ ४२ ॥
verses.transliteration: taṁ vicakṣya khalaṁ putraṁśāsanair vividhair nṛpaḥyadā na śāsituṁ kalpobhṛśam āsīt sudurmanāḥ
verses.text: After seeing the cruel and merciless behavior of his son, Vena, King Aṅga punished him in different ways to reform him, but was unable to bring him to the path of gentleness. He thus became greatly aggrieved.

verses.sanskrit: प्रायेणाभ्यर्चितो देवो येऽप्रजा गृहमेधिन: ।कदपत्यभृतं दु:खं ये न विन्दन्ति दुर्भरम् ॥ ४३ ॥
verses.transliteration: prāyeṇābhyarcito devoye ’prajā gṛha-medhinaḥkad-apatya-bhṛtaṁ duḥkhaṁye na vindanti durbharam
verses.text: The King thought to himself: Persons who have no son are certainly fortunate. They must have worshiped the Lord in their previous lives so that they would not have to suffer the unbearable unhappiness caused by a bad son.

verses.sanskrit: यत: पापीयसी कीर्तिरधर्मश्च महान्नृणाम् ।यतो विरोध: सर्वेषां यत आधिरनन्तक: ॥ ४४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yataḥ pāpīyasī kīrtiradharmaś ca mahān nṛṇāmyato virodhaḥ sarveṣāṁyata ādhir anantakaḥ
verses.text: A sinful son causes a person’s reputation to vanish. His irreligious activities at home cause irreligion and quarrel among everyone, and this creates only endless anxiety.

verses.sanskrit: कस्तं प्रजापदेशं वै मोहबन्धनमात्मन: ।पण्डितो बहु मन्येत यदर्था: क्लेशदा गृहा: ॥ ४५ ॥
verses.transliteration: kas taṁ prajāpadeśaṁ vaimoha-bandhanam ātmanaḥpaṇḍito bahu manyetayad-arthāḥ kleśadā gṛhāḥ
verses.text: Who, if he is considerate and intelligent, would desire such a worthless son? Such a son is nothing but a bond of illusion for the living entity, and he makes one’s home miserable.

verses.sanskrit: कदपत्यं वरं मन्ये सदपत्याच्छुचां पदात् ।निर्विद्येत गृहान्मर्त्यो यत्‍क्‍लेशनिवहा गृहा: ॥ ४६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kad-apatyaṁ varaṁ manyesad-apatyāc chucāṁ padātnirvidyeta gṛhān martyoyat-kleśa-nivahā gṛhāḥ
verses.text: Then the King thought: A bad son is better than a good son because a good son creates an attachment for home, whereas a bad son does not. A bad son creates a hellish home from which an intelligent man naturally becomes very easily detached.

verses.sanskrit: एवं स निर्विण्णमना नृपो गृहा-न्निशीथ उत्थाय महोदयोदयात् ।अलब्धनिद्रोऽनुपलक्षितो नृभि-र्हित्वा गतो वेनसुवं प्रसुप्ताम् ॥ ४७ ॥
verses.transliteration: evaṁ sa nirviṇṇa-manā nṛpo gṛhānniśītha utthāya mahodayodayātalabdha-nidro ’nupalakṣito nṛbhirhitvā gato vena-suvaṁ prasuptām
verses.text: Thinking like that, King Aṅga could not sleep at night. He became completely indifferent to household life. Once, therefore, in the dead of night, he got up from bed and left Vena’s mother [his wife], who was sleeping deeply. He gave up all attraction for his greatly opulent kingdom, and, unseen by anyone, he very silently gave up his home and opulence and proceeded towards the forest.

verses.sanskrit: विज्ञाय निर्विद्य गतं पतिं प्रजा:पुरोहितामात्यसुहृद्गणादय: ।विचिक्युरुर्व्यामतिशोककातरायथा निगूढं पुरुषं कुयोगिन: ॥ ४८ ॥
verses.transliteration: vijñāya nirvidya gataṁ patiṁ prajāḥpurohitāmātya-suhṛd-gaṇādayaḥvicikyur urvyām atiśoka-kātarāyathā nigūḍhaṁ puruṣaṁ kuyoginaḥ
verses.text: When it was understood that the King had indifferently left home, all the citizens, priests, ministers, friends, and people in general were greatly aggrieved. They began to search for him all over the world, just as a less experienced mystic searches out the Supersoul within himself.

verses.sanskrit: अलक्षयन्त: पदवीं प्रजापते-र्हतोद्यमा: प्रत्युपसृत्य ते पुरीम् ।ऋषीन् समेतानभिवन्द्य साश्रवोन्यवेदयन् पौरव भर्तृविप्लवम् ॥ ४९ ॥
verses.transliteration: alakṣayantaḥ padavīṁ prajāpaterhatodyamāḥ pratyupasṛtya te purīmṛṣīn sametān abhivandya sāśravonyavedayan paurava bhartṛ-viplavam
verses.text: When the citizens could not find any trace of the King after searching for him everywhere, they were very disappointed, and they returned to the city, where all the great sages of the country assembled because of the King’s absence. With tears in their eyes the citizens offered respectful obeisances and informed the sages in full detail that they were unable to find the King anywhere.
